packages.name: moment
packages.synopsis: Package moment provides a moment.js-style date and time API layered on top of the Go standard library's time package.
packages.doc:
Package moment provides a moment.js-style date and time API layered on top of
the Go standard library's time package. It is implemented purely with the
standard library: no cgo and no third-party dependencies.

# The Moment type

A Moment is an immutable wrapper around time.Time. Every manipulation method
returns a new Moment and never mutates the receiver, so values are safe to
share. Construct one with New, FromTime, Now, Unix, UnixMilli, DateTime, or
by parsing a string:

	m := moment.Now()
	m := moment.Unix(1500000000)
	m, err := moment.Parse("2017-07-14T02:40:00Z")
	m, err := moment.ParseFormat("14/07/2017", "DD/MM/YYYY")

# Deterministic clock

Relative-time helpers such as FromNow and Calendar consult a Clock. The
default is backed by time.Now, but tests can inject a fixed clock so the
output is deterministic:

	clock := moment.FixedClock(reference)
	m := moment.New(t).WithClock(clock)
	fmt.Println(m.FromNow())

# Format tokens

Format and ParseFormat use moment.js-style tokens which are translated to Go
reference layouts internally. The supported tokens are:

	YYYY  four-digit year        2006
	YY    two-digit year         06
	MMMM  full month name        January
	MMM   short month name       Jan
	MM    two-digit month        01
	M     month                  1
	DD    two-digit day          02
	D     day of month           2
	dddd  full weekday name      Monday
	ddd   short weekday name     Mon
	HH    two-digit 24h hour     15
	H     24h hour               (padded; Go has no unpadded form)
	hh    two-digit 12h hour     03
	h     12h hour               3
	mm    two-digit minute       04
	m     minute                 4
	ss    two-digit second       05
	s     second                 5
	SSS   milliseconds           000
	A     upper meridiem         PM
	a     lower meridiem         pm
	Z     numeric zone offset    -07:00
	ZZ    numeric zone offset    -0700

Text wrapped in square brackets is emitted literally, for example
"YYYY [year]". FormatLayout and ParseLayout accept a raw Go layout instead.

# Manipulation

Add and Subtract move a Moment by a number of units; StartOf and EndOf snap
to unit boundaries; Set replaces a single component; Clone copies the value.
Units are the Unit constants (Year, Month, Week, Day, Hour, Minute, Second,
Millisecond) and common moment.js aliases such as "days" or "h" are accepted.

	m.Add(3, moment.Day)
	m.Subtract(1, moment.Month)
	m.StartOf(moment.Month)
	m.EndOf(moment.Year)
	m.Set(moment.Hour, 9)

# Comparison and query

IsBefore, IsAfter, IsSame, IsSameOrBefore, IsSameOrAfter and IsBetween
compare Moments. Getters include Year, Month, Date, Hour, Minute, Second,
Millisecond, Weekday, DayOfYear and ISOWeek.

# Difference and relative time

Diff reports the signed difference between two Moments in any unit as a
float64; DiffInt truncates toward zero and DiffDuration returns a
time.Duration. FromNow, From, To and ToNow produce humanized phrases such as
"in 3 days" or "2 hours ago", Humanize renders a bare duration, and Calendar
yields strings like "Today at 2:30 PM".

# Time zones

In, UTC and Local reinterpret a Moment in another time zone using
time.Location values obtained from time.UTC, time.Local or
time.LoadLocation. UTCOffset and SetUTCOffset get and set the zone offset in
minutes, and IsDST reports daylight-saving time.

# Locales

Format, Calendar, FromNow and the humanized relative-time helpers are
locale-aware. Select a locale per Moment with Locale, or set the process-wide
default with SetGlobalLocale. A representative set of about twenty common
locales is bundled (en, en-gb, fr, de, es, it, pt, pt-br, nl, ru, zh-cn,
zh-tw, ja, ko, ar, hi, tr, pl, sv, cs); the full moment.js catalogue of ~140
locales is not bundled, but the Locale type and RegisterLocale let you add
any locale you need. See AvailableLocales for the shipped set.

	m.Locale("fr").Format("LLLL") // mardi 4 juillet 2017 14:05
	m.Locale("de").FromNow()      // in 3 Tagen

The full moment token set is supported by Format and ParseFormat, including
Q/Qo, Do, DDD/DDDo, w/wo/ww, W/Wo/WW, e/E, gg/gggg, GG/GGGG, k/kk, x/X, z/zz,
runs of S for fractional seconds, and the long-date tokens LT, LTS, L, LL,
LLL and LLLL.

# Durations

Duration is the moment.js duration analogue. Construct one with NewDuration
or DurationBetween, convert it with As, read components with Get, do
arithmetic with Add and Subtract, humanize it with Humanize, and serialize it
to and from ISO-8601 with ISOString and ParseDuration.

	d := moment.NewDuration(90, moment.Minute)
	d.AsHours()        // 1.5
	d.Humanize(true)   // in an hour
	d.ISOString()      // PT1H30M

# Parsing and validity

Beyond Parse (ISO/RFC auto-detection), ParseFormat parses moment tokens,
ParseFormatStrict requires an exact match, ParseFormats tries a list of
formats, and ParseRFC2822, ParseISO and ParseDuration handle their named
formats. FromArray and FromObject build a Moment from components. A failed
parse yields an invalid Moment (see Invalid, Moment.IsValid and
Moment.CreationData).

types.name: CalendarFormats
types.signature:
type CalendarFormats struct {
	SameDay  string
	NextDay  string
	NextWeek string
	LastDay  string
	LastWeek string
	SameElse string
}
types.doc:
CalendarFormats holds the moment.js calendar templates used by Calendar to
describe a Moment relative to a reference day. Each value is a format string
(tokens plus [bracketed] literals), for example "[Today at] LT".

types.name: Clock
types.doc:
Clock is the source of the current time. It is injected into Moment values so
that relative-time helpers such as FromNow and Calendar are deterministic in
tests. The standard implementation is backed by time.Now.

funcs.name: FixedClock
funcs.doc:
FixedClock returns a Clock that always reports the same instant. It is handy
for deterministic tests.

types.name: ClockFunc
types.doc: ClockFunc adapts an ordinary function to the Clock interface.

methods.name: Now
methods.doc: Now calls the underlying function.

types.name: CreationData
types.doc:
CreationData records how a Moment was constructed by a parsing function,
mirroring moment.js's creationData(). It is returned by Moment.CreationData.

types.name: DateSpec
types.doc:
DateSpec holds calendar and clock components used by SetAll and FromObject.
A nil pointer leaves that component unchanged (SetAll) or at its default
(FromObject). Month is 1-based (time.January == 1).

types.name: Duration
types.doc:
Duration is a length of time, the moment.js Duration analogue. It stores
months, days and milliseconds separately (because months and days have
variable lengths) and offers conversion (As), component access (Get),
arithmetic, ISO-8601 serialization and locale-aware humanization.

funcs.name: DurationBetween
funcs.doc:
DurationBetween returns the Duration from from to to (that is, to − from),
measured in milliseconds.

funcs.name: DurationFromTime
funcs.doc:
DurationFromTime returns a Duration equal to the given time.Duration, stored
entirely as milliseconds.

funcs.name: NewDuration
funcs.signature: func NewDuration(n int, unit Unit) Duration
funcs.doc:
NewDuration returns a Duration of n of the given unit. Recognized units are
Year, Quarter, Month, Week, Day, Hour, Minute, Second and Millisecond (plus
their moment.js aliases).

funcs.name: NewDurationFromObject
funcs.doc:
NewDurationFromObject builds a Duration from a map of unit names to amounts,
mirroring moment.js's moment.duration({...}) object form. Recognized keys are
the moment.js unit names and their aliases, for example "years"/"y",
"months"/"M", "weeks"/"w", "days"/"d", "hours"/"h", "minutes"/"m",
"seconds"/"s" and "milliseconds"/"ms". Unknown keys are ignored; an empty map
yields a zero Duration.

funcs.name: ParseDuration
funcs.doc:
ParseDuration parses an ISO-8601 duration string (for example
"P1Y2M10DT2H30M" or "PT1.5S") into a Duration. Weeks (W) are folded into
days. It returns an error for input that is not a valid ISO-8601 duration.

methods.name: Abs
methods.doc: Abs returns the Duration with all components made non-negative.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the sum of the two Durations, component by component.

methods.name: As
methods.doc: As returns the whole Duration expressed in the given unit as a float64.

methods.name: AsDays
methods.doc: AsDays returns the Duration in fractional days.

methods.name: AsHours
methods.doc: AsHours returns the Duration in fractional hours.

methods.name: AsMilliseconds
methods.doc: AsMilliseconds returns the Duration in whole milliseconds.

methods.name: AsMinutes
methods.doc: AsMinutes returns the Duration in fractional minutes.

methods.name: AsMonths
methods.doc: AsMonths returns the Duration in fractional months.

methods.name: AsSeconds
methods.doc: AsSeconds returns the Duration in fractional seconds.

methods.name: AsWeeks
methods.doc: AsWeeks returns the Duration in fractional weeks.

methods.name: AsYears
methods.doc: AsYears returns the Duration in fractional years.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent copy of the Duration.

methods.name: Days
methods.doc: Days returns the days component.

methods.name: Get
methods.doc:
Get returns the Duration's value for the given unit as a normalized component
(for example, Get(Minute) is 0–59, and Get(Month) is 0–11).

methods.name: Hours
methods.doc: Hours returns the hours component (0–23).

methods.name: Humanize
methods.doc:
Humanize renders the Duration as a locale-aware phrase such as "2 days". When
withSuffix is true the locale's future/past wrapper is applied, yielding
phrases like "in 2 days" or "2 days ago".

methods.name: ISOString
methods.doc:
ISOString serializes the Duration as an ISO-8601 duration such as
"P1Y2M10DT2H30M". A zero Duration is "P0D". The output uses the stored
months, days and milliseconds without converting between them. Matching
moment.js, a mixed-sign Duration carries an overall sign taken from the
total length, and any component group whose own sign differs from that total
is rendered with its own leading minus, e.g. {years:-1, hours:1} renders as
"-P1YT-1H".

methods.name: Locale
methods.doc:
Locale returns a copy of the Duration bound to the named locale, affecting
Humanize. An unknown name falls back to the global default.

methods.name: Milliseconds
methods.doc: Milliseconds returns the milliseconds component (0–999).

methods.name: Minutes
methods.doc: Minutes returns the minutes component (0–59).

methods.name: Months
methods.doc: Months returns the months component (0–11).

methods.name: Seconds
methods.doc: Seconds returns the seconds component (0–59).

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns the Duration's ISO-8601 representation.

methods.name: Subtract
methods.doc: Subtract returns d minus other, component by component.

methods.name: ToDuration
methods.doc:
ToDuration returns the Duration as a time.Duration (nanosecond precision from
its total-milliseconds length).

methods.name: ToJSON
methods.doc:
ToJSON returns the Duration serialized for JSON as an ISO-8601 duration
string, mirroring moment.js's Duration.toJSON (which aliases toISOString).

methods.name: ValueOf
methods.doc:
ValueOf returns the Duration's total length in whole milliseconds, mirroring
moment.js's Duration.valueOf. Month and day components are converted using
the same mean-month length As uses, then truncated toward zero.

methods.name: Weeks
methods.doc: Weeks returns the whole-weeks component derived from the days component.

methods.name: Years
methods.doc: Years returns the years component (Get(Year)).

types.name: Locale
types.doc:
Locale describes everything language- and region-specific about rendering and
parsing a Moment: month and weekday names, the meridiem, ordinals, the week
numbering rules, and the relative-time, calendar and long-date templates. Use
RegisterLocale to make a Locale available by name and Moment.Locale to select
one per value.

funcs.name: LookupLocale
funcs.doc:
LookupLocale returns the registered Locale for name and whether it was found.
Lookup is case-insensitive and falls back from a regional variant to its base
language (for example "en-au" falls back to "en").

types.name: LongDateFormats
types.signature:
type LongDateFormats struct {
	LT   string // time, e.g. "h:mm A"
	LTS  string // time with seconds, e.g. "h:mm:ss A"
	L    string // numeric date, e.g. "MM/DD/YYYY"
	LL   string // long date, e.g. "MMMM D, YYYY"
	LLL  string // long date and time, e.g. "MMMM D, YYYY h:mm A"
	LLLL string // full date and time, e.g. "dddd, MMMM D, YYYY h:mm A"
}
types.doc:
LongDateFormats holds the locale's named long date/time patterns, referenced
from format strings and calendar templates by the tokens LT, LTS, L, LL, LLL
and LLLL.

types.name: Moment
types.doc:
Moment is an immutable wrapper around time.Time offering a moment.js-style
API for parsing, formatting, manipulation and comparison. All manipulation
methods return a new Moment and never mutate the receiver.

funcs.name: DateTime
funcs.signature: func DateTime(year int, month time.Month, day, hour, min, sec, nsec int, loc *time.Location) Moment
funcs.doc:
DateTime builds a Moment from calendar components in the given location. A
nil location is treated as UTC.

funcs.name: FromArray
funcs.doc:
FromArray builds a Moment from a component slice in UTC, following moment.js's
array form [year, month, day, hour, minute, second, millisecond]. As in
moment, the month is 0-based (0 == January). Missing trailing components
default to the start of their range (day and month default to 1). An empty
slice yields an invalid Moment.

funcs.name: FromArrayInLocation
funcs.doc:
FromArrayInLocation is FromArray in the given location. A nil location is
treated as UTC.

funcs.name: FromObject
funcs.doc:
FromObject builds a Moment from a map of component names to values in UTC,
mirroring moment.js's object form. Recognized keys are "year"/"years"/"y",
"month"/"months"/"M" (0-based), "day"/"days"/"date"/"D", "hour"/"hours"/"h",
"minute"/"minutes"/"m", "second"/"seconds"/"s" and
"millisecond"/"milliseconds"/"ms". Unset components default to the start of
their range.

funcs.name: FromObjectInLocation
funcs.doc:
FromObjectInLocation is FromObject in the given location. A nil location is
treated as UTC.

funcs.name: FromTime
funcs.doc: FromTime is an alias for New, wrapping a time.Time value.

funcs.name: Invalid
funcs.doc:
Invalid returns an explicitly invalid Moment, the moral equivalent of
moment.invalid(). Its IsValid reports false and Format returns "Invalid
date".

funcs.name: Max
funcs.doc:
Max returns the latest of the given Moments. Invalid Moments are ignored;
if no valid Moment is supplied the result is Invalid.

funcs.name: Min
funcs.doc:
Min returns the earliest of the given Moments. Invalid Moments are ignored;
if no valid Moment is supplied the result is Invalid.

funcs.name: New
funcs.doc: New wraps an existing time.Time in a Moment using the system clock.

funcs.name: Now
funcs.doc: Now returns a Moment for the current instant according to the system clock.

funcs.name: NowWith
funcs.doc:
NowWith returns a Moment for the current instant according to the supplied
clock. The returned Moment carries that clock so relative-time helpers stay
deterministic.

funcs.name: Parse
funcs.doc:
Parse attempts to interpret value using a list of common ISO-8601 and RFC
layouts. It returns an error if none of them match.

funcs.name: ParseFormat
funcs.doc:
ParseFormat parses value using a moment.js-style token format string in the
Moment's global locale, interpreting zone-less input as UTC.

funcs.name: ParseFormatLocale
funcs.signature: func ParseFormatLocale(value, format, locale string) (Moment, error)
funcs.doc:
ParseFormatLocale parses value using format and the named locale (so that
localized month and weekday names are recognized).

funcs.name: ParseFormatStrict
funcs.doc:
ParseFormatStrict parses value against format in strict mode: the value must
match the format exactly, with no extra or missing characters.

funcs.name: ParseFormats
funcs.doc:
ParseFormats tries each format in turn (moment.js's multiple-format parsing)
and returns the first that succeeds. It returns an error if none match.

funcs.name: ParseISO
funcs.doc:
ParseISO parses an ISO-8601 date/time string. It is a synonym for Parse that
documents intent when the input is known to be ISO-8601.

funcs.name: ParseInLocation
funcs.signature: func ParseInLocation(value, format string, loc *time.Location) (Moment, error)
funcs.doc:
ParseInLocation parses value using a moment-style token format, interpreting
zone-less input as being in loc. A nil location is treated as UTC.

funcs.name: ParseLayout
funcs.doc: ParseLayout parses value using a raw Go reference layout.

funcs.name: ParseRFC2822
funcs.doc:
ParseRFC2822 parses an RFC 2822 formatted date, e.g.
"Tue, 01 Nov 2016 13:23:12 +0630".

funcs.name: ParseZone
funcs.doc:
ParseZone parses an ISO-8601 or RFC date/time string and keeps the UTC offset
carried by the input, mirroring moment.js's moment.parseZone. Whereas some
constructors normalise to UTC or local time, the Moment returned here is
pinned to a fixed zone equal to the parsed offset, so Format, ISO and
UTCOffset report that offset. Inputs without an explicit offset are treated as
UTC. It returns an invalid Moment and an error when no layout matches.

funcs.name: Unix
funcs.doc: Unix returns a Moment for the given Unix timestamp in seconds, in UTC.

funcs.name: UnixMilli
funcs.doc:
UnixMilli returns a Moment for the given Unix timestamp in milliseconds, in
UTC.

methods.name: Add
methods.signature: func (m Moment) Add(n int, unit Unit) Moment
methods.doc:
Add returns a new Moment advanced by n of the given unit. Calendar units
(years, months, weeks, days) use civil arithmetic; year, quarter and month
arithmetic clamps the day of the month like moment.js (January 31 + 1 month
is February 28/29). Clock units use fixed durations.

methods.name: AddDuration
methods.doc: AddDuration returns a new Moment advanced by an arbitrary time.Duration.

methods.name: Calendar
methods.doc:
Calendar returns a locale-aware calendar string describing m relative to
reference, such as "Today at 2:30 PM" or "Last Friday at 9:00 AM". Days
outside a one-week window fall back to the locale's numeric date (sameElse).

methods.name: CalendarNow
methods.doc: CalendarNow is Calendar relative to the Moment's clock.

methods.name: CalendarWith
methods.signature: func (m Moment) CalendarWith(reference Moment, formats CalendarFormats) string
methods.doc:
CalendarWith is Calendar with caller-supplied templates, mirroring moment.js's
calendar(referenceDay, formats). Each non-empty field of formats overrides the
corresponding locale template for that bucket (same day, next day, and so on);
empty fields fall back to the Moment locale's default calendar templates. This
lets callers customise the phrasing without registering a whole locale.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent copy of the Moment.

methods.name: CreationData
methods.doc:
CreationData returns how the Moment was produced by a parsing constructor, or
nil when it was not created by one.

methods.name: Date
methods.doc: Date returns the day of the month (1-31).

methods.name: Day
methods.doc: Day is an alias for Date, returning the day of the month.

methods.name: DayOfYear
methods.doc: DayOfYear returns the 1-based day of the year (1-366).

methods.name: DaysInMonth
methods.doc: DaysInMonth returns the number of days in the Moment's month (28–31).

methods.name: DaysInYear
methods.doc:
DaysInYear returns the number of days in the Moment's year: 366 in a leap
year and 365 otherwise.

methods.name: Diff
methods.signature: func (m Moment) Diff(other Moment, unit Unit) float64
methods.doc:
Diff returns the signed difference m - other expressed in the given unit as a
floating-point value. Years and months use civil arithmetic; smaller units
use exact durations.

methods.name: DiffDuration
methods.doc: DiffDuration returns the signed difference m - other as a time.Duration.

methods.name: DiffInt
methods.signature: func (m Moment) DiffInt(other Moment, unit Unit) int
methods.doc:
DiffInt returns Diff truncated toward zero, matching moment.js's default
integer diff behaviour.

methods.name: EndOf
methods.doc: EndOf returns a new Moment set to the last nanosecond of the given unit.

methods.name: Format
methods.doc:
Format renders the Moment using a moment.js-style token string, resolving
tokens against the Moment's effective locale. Text wrapped in square brackets
is emitted literally, e.g. "YYYY [year]".

methods.name: FormatLayout
methods.doc:
FormatLayout renders the Moment using a raw Go reference layout
(for example "2006-01-02 15:04:05").

methods.name: From
methods.doc:
From returns a humanized, locale-aware description of m relative to other,
for example "in 3 days" or "2 hours ago".

methods.name: FromNow
methods.doc:
FromNow returns a humanized description of m relative to the current time as
reported by the Moment's clock.

methods.name: Get
methods.doc:
Get returns a single component of the Moment identified by unit, the getter
companion to Set and the analogue of moment.js's get. Recognized units are
Year, Quarter, Month, Week, ISOWeek, Day/Date, DayOfYear, Hour, Minute,
Second and Millisecond (plus their aliases). Unlike moment's 0-based month,
Get(Month) returns the 1-based month to stay consistent with Month and
Set(Month, …). Unknown units yield 0.

methods.name: Hour
methods.doc: Hour returns the hour within the day (0-23).

methods.name: ISO
methods.doc:
ISO returns the RFC3339 (ISO-8601) representation of the Moment, preserving
its location and any fractional seconds.

methods.name: ISOWeek
methods.doc: ISOWeek returns the ISO 8601 year and week number.

methods.name: ISOWeekNumber
methods.doc: ISOWeekNumber returns just the ISO 8601 week number (1–53).

methods.name: ISOWeekYear
methods.doc:
ISOWeekYear returns the ISO 8601 week-numbering year (moment's GGGG), which
can differ from the calendar year near the start or end of a year.

methods.name: ISOWeekday
methods.doc:
ISOWeekday returns the ISO 8601 day of the week: 1 for Monday through 7 for
Sunday.

methods.name: ISOWeeksInYear
methods.doc:
ISOWeeksInYear returns the number of ISO weeks (52 or 53) in the Moment's ISO
week-year.

methods.name: In
methods.doc:
In returns a copy of the Moment representing the same instant in the given
location. A nil location is treated as UTC.

methods.name: IsAfter
methods.doc: IsAfter reports whether m is strictly after other.

methods.name: IsBefore
methods.doc: IsBefore reports whether m is strictly before other.

methods.name: IsBetween
methods.signature: func (m Moment) IsBetween(start, end Moment, inclusive bool) bool
methods.doc:
IsBetween reports whether m lies within (start, end). When inclusive is true
the bounds themselves count as inside the range. The bounds may be supplied
in either order.

methods.name: IsDST
methods.doc:
IsDST reports whether the Moment falls in daylight saving time, detected by
comparing its offset with the standard offsets in January and July.

methods.name: IsLeapYear
methods.doc: IsLeapYear reports whether the Moment falls in a leap year.

methods.name: IsLocal
methods.doc:
IsLocal reports whether the Moment's location is the system local zone,
mirroring moment.js's isLocal.

methods.name: IsSame
methods.doc: IsSame reports whether m and other represent the same instant.

methods.name: IsSameOrAfter
methods.doc: IsSameOrAfter reports whether m is after or equal to other.

methods.name: IsSameOrBefore
methods.doc: IsSameOrBefore reports whether m is before or equal to other.

methods.name: IsSameUnit
methods.signature: func (m Moment) IsSameUnit(other Moment, unit Unit) bool
methods.doc:
IsSameUnit reports whether m and other fall in the same calendar unit (for
example the same day or the same month), comparing after truncation.

methods.name: IsUTC
methods.doc:
IsUTC reports whether the Moment's location is UTC, mirroring moment.js's
isUTC.

methods.name: IsValid
methods.doc:
IsValid reports whether the Moment holds a usable instant. It is false for
the zero value and for Moments returned by failed parses (see Invalid),
mirroring moment.js's isValid.

methods.name: IsZero
methods.doc: IsZero reports whether the underlying instant is the zero time.

methods.name: Local
methods.doc: Local returns a copy of the Moment in the local location.

methods.name: Locale
methods.doc:
Locale returns a copy of the Moment bound to the named locale, affecting
Format, Calendar, FromNow and the humanized relative-time helpers. An unknown
name leaves the Moment on the global default; use LookupLocale to test first.

methods.name: LocaleData
methods.doc:
LocaleData returns the effective Locale for the Moment: its bound locale, or
the global default. The returned pointer must not be mutated.

methods.name: LocaleName
methods.doc:
LocaleName returns the name of the locale bound to the Moment, or the global
default when none is set.

methods.name: LocaleWeekday
methods.doc:
LocaleWeekday returns the day of the week numbered from the Moment locale's
first day of the week (0 == the locale's first weekday), matching moment.js's
locale-aware weekday getter. Compare ISOWeekday, which is always Monday-based.

methods.name: Location
methods.doc: Location returns the time zone associated with the Moment.

methods.name: Millisecond
methods.doc: Millisecond returns the millisecond offset within the second (0-999).

methods.name: Minute
methods.doc: Minute returns the minute offset within the hour (0-59).

methods.name: Month
methods.doc: Month returns the month.

methods.name: Nanosecond
methods.doc: Nanosecond returns the nanosecond offset within the second.

methods.name: Quarter
methods.doc: Quarter returns the calendar quarter of the year (1–4).

methods.name: Second
methods.doc: Second returns the second offset within the minute (0-59).

methods.name: Set
methods.signature: func (m Moment) Set(unit Unit, value int) Moment
methods.doc:
Set returns a new Moment with a single component replaced by value. The
Quarter unit keeps the month's position within the quarter; DayOfYear sets
the 1-based day of the year. Changing the year, quarter or month clamps the
day of the month like moment.js: setting May 31 to April yields April 30
rather than overflowing into May.

methods.name: SetAll
methods.doc: SetAll returns a new Moment with every non-nil component of spec applied.

methods.name: SetDate
methods.doc:
SetDate returns a new Moment with the day of the month replaced by d, the
setter companion to Date and the analogue of moment.js's date(value).

methods.name: SetDayOfYear
methods.doc:
SetDayOfYear returns a new Moment set to the d-th day of its year (1-based),
matching moment.js's dayOfYear(value).

methods.name: SetHour
methods.doc:
SetHour returns a new Moment with the hour replaced by h, matching
moment.js's hour(value).

methods.name: SetISOWeekday
methods.doc:
SetISOWeekday returns a new Moment moved to ISO weekday d (1 == Monday, 7 ==
Sunday) within the same ISO week, matching moment.js's isoWeekday(value).

methods.name: SetMillisecond
methods.doc:
SetMillisecond returns a new Moment with the millisecond replaced by ms,
matching moment.js's millisecond(value).

methods.name: SetMinute
methods.doc:
SetMinute returns a new Moment with the minute replaced by mi, matching
moment.js's minute(value).

methods.name: SetMonth
methods.doc:
SetMonth returns a new Moment with the month replaced by mo, the setter
companion to Month and the analogue of moment.js's month(value). Overflow
beyond a month's length rolls forward as in time.Date.

methods.name: SetQuarter
methods.doc:
SetQuarter returns a new Moment moved to quarter q, matching moment.js's
quarter(value). Following moment, the month keeps its position within the
quarter (its month-mod-3 offset) rather than snapping to the first month, and
values outside 1–4 bubble the year forward or backward.

methods.name: SetSecond
methods.doc:
SetSecond returns a new Moment with the second replaced by s, matching
moment.js's second(value).

methods.name: SetUTCOffset
methods.doc:
SetUTCOffset returns a new Moment shifted to the given zone offset (in
minutes east of UTC) while representing the same instant. It is the setter
companion to the UTCOffset getter.

methods.name: SetWeekday
methods.doc:
SetWeekday returns a new Moment moved to weekday w within the same
Sunday-based week, matching moment.js's day(value): the result stays in the
current week and only the day of the week changes.

methods.name: SetYear
methods.doc:
SetYear returns a new Moment with the year replaced by y, the setter
companion to Year and the analogue of moment.js's year(value).

methods.name: StartOf
methods.doc:
StartOf returns a new Moment truncated to the start of the given unit,
preserving the time zone. The Week unit honours the Moment's locale first day
of the week; ISOWeek always starts on Monday.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String implements fmt.Stringer, returning the ISO-8601 representation.

methods.name: Subtract
methods.signature: func (m Moment) Subtract(n int, unit Unit) Moment
methods.doc: Subtract returns a new Moment moved back by n of the given unit.

methods.name: Time
methods.doc: Time returns the underlying time.Time value.

methods.name: To
methods.doc:
To returns a humanized description of other relative to m. It is the inverse
of From.

methods.name: ToArray
methods.doc:
ToArray returns the Moment's calendar and clock components as a slice in the
moment.js toArray order: [year, month, day, hour, minute, second,
millisecond]. As in moment.js the month is 0-based (0 == January), matching
FromArray. An invalid Moment yields nil.

methods.name: ToDate
methods.doc:
ToDate returns the underlying time.Time value, mirroring moment.js's
toDate(). It is an alias for Time.

methods.name: ToISOString
methods.doc:
ToISOString returns the moment.js toISOString representation: the instant in
UTC with millisecond precision and a trailing "Z", e.g.
"2017-07-14T02:40:00.000Z".

methods.name: ToISOStringZone
methods.doc:
ToISOStringZone returns the moment.js toISOString(true) representation: the
instant with millisecond precision but keeping the Moment's own zone offset
rather than converting to UTC, e.g. "2017-07-14T02:40:00.000-05:00". An
invalid Moment yields the empty string.

methods.name: ToJSON
methods.doc:
ToJSON returns the Moment serialized for JSON as a UTC ISO-8601 string with
millisecond precision, matching moment.js's toJSON (which aliases
toISOString). An invalid Moment yields the empty string, mirroring moment's
null.

methods.name: ToNow
methods.doc: ToNow returns a humanized description of the current time relative to m.

methods.name: ToObject
methods.doc:
ToObject returns the Moment's components keyed by moment.js's toObject names:
"years", "months" (0-based), "date", "hours", "minutes", "seconds" and
"milliseconds". The keys mirror FromObject. An invalid Moment yields nil.

methods.name: UTC
methods.doc: UTC returns a copy of the Moment in the UTC location.

methods.name: UTCOffset
methods.doc: UTCOffset returns the Moment's zone offset east of UTC in minutes.

methods.name: Unix
methods.doc: Unix returns the Moment as a Unix timestamp in seconds.

methods.name: UnixMilli
methods.doc: UnixMilli returns the Moment as a Unix timestamp in milliseconds.

methods.name: ValueOf
methods.doc:
ValueOf returns the Moment as a Unix timestamp in milliseconds, matching
moment.js's valueOf.

methods.name: Week
methods.doc: Week returns the locale-aware week-of-year number under the Moment's locale.

methods.name: WeekYear
methods.doc:
WeekYear returns the locale-aware week-numbering year under the Moment's
locale (moment's gggg).

methods.name: Weekday
methods.doc: Weekday returns the day of the week.

methods.name: WeeksInYear
methods.doc: WeeksInYear returns the number of locale weeks in the Moment's year.

methods.name: WithClock
methods.doc:
WithClock returns a copy of the Moment that uses the supplied clock for
relative-time helpers. A nil clock resets to the system clock.

methods.name: Year
methods.doc: Year returns the year.

methods.name: ZoneAbbr
methods.doc:
ZoneAbbr returns the abbreviated zone name in effect at the Moment, such as
"UTC" or "EST", matching moment.js's zoneAbbr. The abbreviation comes from
the Moment's time.Location and may be a numeric offset for fixed zones.

methods.name: ZoneName
methods.doc:
ZoneName returns the Moment's full time-zone identifier, for example "UTC" or
"America/New_York". It is the loaded location name; use ZoneAbbr for the
short form that moment.js prints.

types.name: RelativeTime
types.signature:
type RelativeTime struct {
	Future  string // wraps a future phrase, e.g. "in %s"
	Past    string // wraps a past phrase, e.g. "%s ago"
	Second  string // key "s"  – a few seconds
	Seconds string // key "ss" – "%d seconds"
	Minute  string // key "m"  – a minute
	Minutes string // key "mm" – "%d minutes"
	Hour    string // key "h"  – an hour
	Hours   string // key "hh" – "%d hours"
	Day     string // key "d"  – a day
	Days    string // key "dd" – "%d days"
	Week    string // key "w"  – a week
	Weeks   string // key "ww" – "%d weeks"
	Month   string // key "M"  – a month
	Months  string // key "MM" – "%d months"
	Year    string // key "y"  – a year
	Years   string // key "yy" – "%d years"

	// Pluralize, when non-nil, overrides the field templates above. It is used
	// by locales with non-trivial plural rules (Slavic languages, Arabic). key
	// is one of "s","ss","m","mm","h","hh","d","dd","w","ww","M","MM","y","yy".
	Pluralize func(number int, withoutSuffix bool, key string, isFuture bool) string
}
types.doc:
RelativeTime holds the phrase templates a locale uses to render humanized
durations such as "in 3 days" or "2 hours ago". Each field corresponds to a
moment.js relative-time key; templates may contain the verb "%d" placeholder,
which is replaced by the magnitude. Future and Past wrap the resulting phrase
(their "%s" placeholder is replaced by the rendered magnitude).

types.name: Unit
types.doc:
Unit identifies a calendar or clock unit used by Add, Subtract, StartOf,
EndOf, Set and Diff.

consts.signature:
const (
	Year        Unit = "year"
	Quarter     Unit = "quarter"
	Month       Unit = "month"
	Week        Unit = "week"
	ISOWeek     Unit = "isoWeek"
	Day         Unit = "day"
	Date        Unit = "date"
	DayOfYear   Unit = "dayOfYear"
	Hour        Unit = "hour"
	Minute      Unit = "minute"
	Second      Unit = "second"
	Millisecond Unit = "millisecond"
)
consts.doc:
Supported units. Manipulation and diff helpers also accept common moment.js
aliases (plural and short forms) via normalizeUnit.

funcs.name: NormalizeUnit
funcs.doc:
NormalizeUnit resolves a moment.js unit alias (plural or short form such as
"days", "d" or "M") to its canonical Unit, reporting whether it was
recognized. It is the analogue of moment.js's moment.normalizeUnits.

funcs.name: AvailableLocales
FirstDayOfWeek
funcs.doc:
FirstDayOfWeek returns the named locale's first day of the week (0 == Sunday,
1 == Monday), mirroring moment.js's localeData().firstDayOfWeek.

funcs.name: FirstWeekContainsDate
funcs.doc:
FirstWeekContainsDate returns the January date that always falls in week 1
under the named locale's week-numbering rules (moment's "doy"), mirroring
localeData().firstDayOfYear.

funcs.name: GlobalLocale
funcs.doc: GlobalLocale returns the name of the current global default locale.

funcs.name: Humanize
funcs.doc:
Humanize renders a bare time.Duration as a human-friendly phrase such as
"2 hours" or "a few seconds", without any past/future suffix, using the
global default locale.

funcs.name: IsDate
funcs.doc:
IsDate reports whether v is a time.Time value, the Go analogue of moment.js's
moment.isDate (which tests for a JavaScript Date).

funcs.name: IsDuration
funcs.doc:
IsDuration reports whether v is a Duration value, mirroring moment.js's
moment.isDuration.

funcs.name: IsMoment
funcs.doc:
IsMoment reports whether v is a Moment value, mirroring moment.js's
moment.isMoment.

funcs.name: LongDateFormat
funcs.doc:
LongDateFormat returns the named locale's pattern for one of the long-date
tokens LT, LTS, L, LL, LLL or LLLL, mirroring moment.js's
localeData().longDateFormat. An unknown token yields the empty string.

funcs.name: Meridiem
funcs.signature: func Meridiem(locale string, hour, minute int, isLower bool) string
funcs.doc:
Meridiem returns the named locale's meridiem string for the given clock time;
isLower requests the lower-case form. It mirrors moment.js's
localeData().meridiem and honours locales (such as Chinese and Japanese) that
use time-of-day words instead of AM/PM.

funcs.name: MonthName
funcs.signature: func MonthName(locale string, m time.Month) string
funcs.doc: MonthName returns the full name of month m in the named locale.

funcs.name: MonthShortName
funcs.signature: func MonthShortName(locale string, m time.Month) string
funcs.doc: MonthShortName returns the abbreviated name of month m in the named locale.

funcs.name: Months
funcs.doc:
Months returns a copy of the twelve month names (January first) for the named
locale, mirroring moment.js's moment.months(). An unknown locale falls back
to the global default.

funcs.name: MonthsShort
funcs.doc:
MonthsShort returns a copy of the twelve abbreviated month names (January
first) for the named locale, mirroring moment.js's moment.monthsShort().

funcs.name: Ordinal
funcs.signature: func Ordinal(locale string, n int) string
funcs.doc:
Ordinal renders n as an ordinal in the named locale, for example "1st" in
English or "1." in German, mirroring moment.js's localeData().ordinal.

funcs.name: RegisterLocale
funcs.doc:
RegisterLocale adds or replaces a Locale in the global registry, keyed by its
lower-cased Name. It is safe for concurrent use. Registering a locale named
"en" replaces the built-in English default.

funcs.name: RelativeTimeThreshold
funcs.doc:
RelativeTimeThreshold returns the current cutoff for a relative-time unit, or
-1 for an unknown unit.

funcs.name: SetGlobalLocale
funcs.doc:
SetGlobalLocale sets the default locale used by Moments that do not carry an
explicit locale. It reports whether the name was found; an unknown name
leaves the global default unchanged.

funcs.name: SetRelativeTimeThreshold
funcs.signature: func SetRelativeTimeThreshold(unit string, limit int) bool
funcs.doc:
SetRelativeTimeThreshold overrides the cutoff for one relative-time unit, as
moment.relativeTimeThreshold does. Recognized units are "ss", "s", "m", "h",
"d", "w" and "M". It reports whether the unit name was recognized.

funcs.name: SupportedTokens
funcs.doc:
SupportedTokens returns the moment-style format tokens understood by Format
and ParseFormat, ordered longest-first. In addition, a run of "S" characters
renders that many digits of the fractional second.

funcs.name: WeekdayMinName
funcs.signature: func WeekdayMinName(locale string, w time.Weekday) string
funcs.doc: WeekdayMinName returns the minimal name of weekday w in the named locale.

funcs.name: WeekdayName
funcs.signature: func WeekdayName(locale string, w time.Weekday) string
funcs.doc: WeekdayName returns the full name of weekday w in the named locale.

funcs.name: WeekdayShortName
funcs.signature: func WeekdayShortName(locale string, w time.Weekday) string
funcs.doc:
WeekdayShortName returns the abbreviated name of weekday w in the named
locale.

funcs.name: Weekdays
funcs.doc:
Weekdays returns a copy of the seven weekday names (Sunday first, index 0 ==
Sunday) for the named locale, mirroring moment.js's moment.weekdays().

funcs.name: WeekdaysMin
funcs.doc:
WeekdaysMin returns a copy of the seven minimal weekday names (Sunday first)
for the named locale, mirroring moment.js's moment.weekdaysMin().

funcs.name: WeekdaysShort
funcs.doc:
WeekdaysShort returns a copy of the seven abbreviated weekday names (Sunday
first) for the named locale, mirroring moment.js's moment.weekdaysShort().

examples.output:
Friday, July 14, 2017 at 2:40 AM
2017-07-17
2017-07-01T00:00:00Z
in 2 hours
Yesterday at 2:40 AM
examples.doc:
Example demonstrates formatting, manipulation and deterministic relative time
using a fixed clock so the output is stable.

examples.name: Duration
examples.code:
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/malcolmston/moment"
)

func main() {
	d := moment.NewDuration(1, moment.Year).
		Add(moment.NewDuration(2, moment.Month)).
		Add(moment.NewDuration(10, moment.Day))

	fmt.Println(d.ISOString())
	fmt.Println(d.AsMonths() > 14)
	fmt.Println(moment.NewDuration(2, moment.Hour).Humanize(true))

	parsed, _ := moment.ParseDuration("P1Y2M10DT2H30M")
	fmt.Println(parsed.ISOString())

}
examples.doc: ExampleDuration demonstrates durations, conversion and ISO-8601 round-trips.

examples.name: Moment (Locale)
examples.doc: ExampleMoment_Locale shows locale-aware formatting and relative time.
